metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_360
metadata.comment:
Rationale: CTTNBP2 regulates dendritic spine morphology and synaptic activity (Fig 4) by stabilizing the actin actin-binding protein cortactin (CTTN; SynGO ID 223) at synapses (Fig 5 and 7).

28/3/2017 Pim
The referenced figures show an effect on synapse density. The authors state that CTTNBP2 afects both formation of new synapses and the maintenance of exisiting ones:
1. "Therefore, the experiments performed during DIV 12-18 favor a role of CTTNBP2 in **spinogenesis**"
2. "These data suggest that CTTNBP2 is likely also involved in the **maintenance of dendritic spines**"

The approporiate terms for these processed would be:
ad 1: 'regulation of synapse assembly (GO:0051963)'
ad 2: 'maintenance of synapse structure (term not in SynGO)'

One can also use a single more general term to capture both phenomena: 'regulation of synapse organization (GO:0050807)'. Experimental description: - overexpression constructs (myc tagged CTTNBP2 mutatnts) were generated from mouse cDNAs (see Table 1)
- species of miRNA KD effect is **rat** (rat tissues)

9/3/2017 Pim:
-species of cultured neurons (Fig.4,6,7) is **rat**.
-Final annotation made against **rat**
